title: NATO Secretary General Rutte Publicly Dismisses US Withdrawal Fears, Reaffirms Transatlantic Commitment
detail: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte publicly dismissed fears of a US withdrawal from the alliance on April 19, reaffirming the transatlantic commitment amid ongoing strains between Washington and European allies. Rutte's statement comes days after former Secretary General Stoltenberg warned that NATO's existence is 'not a natural law' and amid Trump's escalating rhetoric questioning allied obligations. The Secretary General's reassurances aim to stabilize alliance cohesion ahead of the Helsingborg Foreign Ministers summit (May 21–22) and in the context of European capitals drafting contingency plans for NATO operations without US support. The statement represents an effort to counter the deepening perception gap between Washington's ambivalence and European anxiety about the alliance's future.
